meaning: n.联合国贸易与发展会议
examples: If understanding prevails, UNCTAD is in a position to replace confrontation with agreement; it cannot be put off.
UNCTAD expects world output to grow by around three percent this year. That is almost one percentage point less than in 2007.
A major idea in the UNCTAD report is its call for the development of a better world financial system with a more secure base.
Despite the fall in overall investment, Unctad said the influence of transnational corporations was continuing to grow.
UNCTAD's project on market access, notes that poor countries often fail to fill Europe's existing preferential import quotas.
